description.en: Commonly cultivated in Sri Lanka's Dry Zone, particularly along roadsides, field edges and occasionally in orchards. Large thorny tree reaches 9 m with pinnate citrus-scented leaves. Hard-shelled round pale-brown fruit cracks open to reveal sticky brown pulp with small white seeds — used for *divul kiri* (a yogurt-like refreshment), curries, juices, and chutneys. Two size classes in markets: larger fruits (sweeter, more valuable) and smaller fruits (acidic, less preferred). Tree prefers dry climates for flowering and fruit set.

description.quote: Wood apples, or Divul (Limonia acidissima of the family Rutaceae), are commonly cultivated in the country's dry zones of Sri Lanka. Limonia acidissima is a large tree growing to 9 metres (30 ft) tall with rough, spiny bark. The round, pale brown fruit has a distinctive taste and a musky aroma. It has a hard, woody outer appearance; the fruit is cracked open to reveal a sticky brown pulp with tiny white seeds inside.
